If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.","items":{"type":"string"},"minItems":1,"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"remove_labels"},{"description":"Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"assignee":{"description":"Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.","type":"string"},"assignees":{"description":"GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). Properties: `Description`, `DefaultValue` (BinaryData), `Schema` (IDictionary\\\\(string, BinaryData\\\\)), `IsRequired`.\\n- **New `StructuredInputs` property on `DeclarativeAgentDefinition`** — `IDictionary` allowing agents to define named structured inputs.\\n- **New `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations`** value added to the protocol enum.\\n- **`AgentDefinitionOptInKeys.ContainerAgentsV1Preview`** — new opt-in key for container agent previews.\\n- **Updated `ProjectsAgentsModelFactory`** with new factory methods for `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` (now includes `structuredInputs`) and a new `StructuredInputDefinition` factory method for mocking.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `StructuredInputDefinition` is a new public API with no documentation, examples, or mention in the README.\\n- The `StructuredInputs` property on `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` is not mentioned in the README or the existing `DeclarativeAgents` section.\\n- The `CHANGELOG.md` for version `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has empty `### Features Added` and other sections — none of the above changes are listed.\\n- No sample test file demonstrates how to use `StructuredInputDefinition` for prompt template substitution.\\n- `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations` (new enum value) is undocumented.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: Update CHANGELOG.md\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nReplace the empty `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added `StructuredInputDefinition` model class to define structured inputs for prompt template substitution and tool argument bindings. Properties include `Description`, `DefaultValue`, `Schema`, and `IsRequired`.\\n- Added `StructuredInputs` property (`IDictionary`) to `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` to allow declarative agents to declare typed, named inputs.\\n- Added `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations` enum value.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: Update README.md\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md`\\n\\n1. In the table of contents under `Examples`, add:\\n   ```\\n     - [Declarative Agents with Structured Inputs](#declarative-agents-with-structured-inputs)\\n   ```\\n\\n2. After the `## Declarative Agents` section (after the code block and explanation), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Declarative Agents with Structured Inputs\\n\\n`StructuredInputDefinition` lets you declare typed, named inputs on a `DeclarativeAgentDefinition`. These inputs can be used in prompt template substitution and tool argument bindings, allowing callers to supply values at invocation time.\\n\\n```\\nDeclarativeAgentDefinition agentDefinition = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant. The user\\u0026#39;s name is \\\\{\\\\{name}} and their preferred language is \\\\{\\\\{language}}.\\u0026#34;,\\n    StructuredInputs =\\n    {\\n        [\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;] = new StructuredInputDefinition\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The name of the user\\u0026#34;,\\n            IsRequired = true\\n        },\\n        [\\u0026#34;language\\u0026#34;] = new StructuredInputDefinition\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The user\\u0026#39;s preferred programming language\\u0026#34;,\\n            DefaultValue = BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;\\\\\\u0026#34;C#\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;),\\n            IsRequired = false\\n        }\\n    }\\n};\\nProjectsAgentVersion agentVersion = await agentsClient.CreateAgentVersionAsync(\\n    agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n    options: new(agentDefinition));\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Agent created (id: {agentVersion.Id}, name: {agentVersion.Name}, version: {agentVersion.Version})\\u0026#34;);\\n` `` `\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite the sample code in a test file so the snippet generator can inject it into the README.\\n\\n**Create or edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_Agents_StructuredInputs.cs`:\\n\\n```\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. The user\\u0026#39;s name is \\\\{\\\\{name}} and their preferred language is \\\\{\\\\{language}}.\\u0026#34;,\\n                StructuredInputs =\\n                {\\n                     IsRequired = false\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            };\\n            ProjectsAgentVersion agentVersion = await agentsClient.CreateAgentVersionAsync(\\n                agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n                options: new(agentDefinition));\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Agent created (id: {agentVersion.Id}, name: {agentVersion.Name}, version: {agentVersion.Version})\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #if !SNIPPET\\n            // Cleanup\\n            await agentsClient.DeleteAgentAsync(agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe README snippet placeholder (added in Step 1) uses Properties: `Description`, `DefaultValue` (BinaryData), `Schema` (IDictionary\\\\(string, BinaryData\\\\)), `IsRequired`.\\n- **New `StructuredInputs` property on `DeclarativeAgentDefinition`** — `IDictionary` allowing agents to define named structured inputs.\\n- **New `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations`** value added to the protocol enum.\\n- **`AgentDefinitionOptInKeys.ContainerAgentsV1Preview`** — new opt-in key for container agent previews.\\n- **Updated `ProjectsAgentsModelFactory`** with new factory methods for `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` (now includes `structuredInputs`) and a new `StructuredInputDefinition` factory method for mocking.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `StructuredInputDefinition` is a new public API with no documentation, examples, or mention in the README.\\n- The `StructuredInputs` property on `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` is not mentioned in the README or the existing `DeclarativeAgents` section.\\n- The `CHANGELOG.md` for version `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` has empty `### Features Added` and other sections — none of the above changes are listed.\\n- No sample test file demonstrates how to use `StructuredInputDefinition` for prompt template substitution.\\n- `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations` (new enum value) is undocumented.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: Update CHANGELOG.md\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nReplace the empty `2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 2.1.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n- Added `StructuredInputDefinition` model class to define structured inputs for prompt template substitution and tool argument bindings. Properties include `Description`, `DefaultValue`, `Schema`, and `IsRequired`.\\n- Added `StructuredInputs` property (`IDictionary`) to `DeclarativeAgentDefinition` to allow declarative agents to declare typed, named inputs.\\n- Added `ProjectsAgentProtocol.Invocations` enum value.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: Update README.md\\n\\n**Edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md`\\n\\n1. In the table of contents under `Examples`, add:\\n   ```\\n     - [Declarative Agents with Structured Inputs](#declarative-agents-with-structured-inputs)\\n   ```\\n\\n2. After the `## Declarative Agents` section (after the code block and explanation), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Declarative Agents with Structured Inputs\\n\\n`StructuredInputDefinition` lets you declare typed, named inputs on a `DeclarativeAgentDefinition`. These inputs can be used in prompt template substitution and tool argument bindings, allowing callers to supply values at invocation time.\\n\\n```\\nDeclarativeAgentDefinition agentDefinition = new(model: modelDeploymentName)\\n{\\n    Instructions = \\u0026#34;You are a helpful assistant. The user\\u0026#39;s name is \\\\{\\\\{name}} and their preferred language is \\\\{\\\\{language}}.\\u0026#34;,\\n    StructuredInputs =\\n    {\\n        [\\u0026#34;name\\u0026#34;] = new StructuredInputDefinition\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The name of the user\\u0026#34;,\\n            IsRequired = true\\n        },\\n        [\\u0026#34;language\\u0026#34;] = new StructuredInputDefinition\\n        {\\n            Description = \\u0026#34;The user\\u0026#39;s preferred programming language\\u0026#34;,\\n            DefaultValue = BinaryData.FromString(\\u0026#34;\\\\\\u0026#34;C#\\\\\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;),\\n            IsRequired = false\\n        }\\n    }\\n};\\nProjectsAgentVersion agentVersion = await agentsClient.CreateAgentVersionAsync(\\n    agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n    options: new(agentDefinition));\\nConsole.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Agent created (id: {agentVersion.Id}, name: {agentVersion.Name}, version: {agentVersion.Version})\\u0026#34;);\\n` `` `\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nWrite the sample code in a test file so the snippet generator can inject it into the README.\\n\\n**Create or edit** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_Agents_StructuredInputs.cs`:\\n\\n```\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. The user\\u0026#39;s name is \\\\{\\\\{name}} and their preferred language is \\\\{\\\\{language}}.\\u0026#34;,\\n                StructuredInputs =\\n                {\\n                     IsRequired = false\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            };\\n            ProjectsAgentVersion agentVersion = await agentsClient.CreateAgentVersionAsync(\\n                agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n                options: new(agentDefinition));\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Agent created (id: {agentVersion.Id}, name: {agentVersion.Name}, version: {agentVersion.Version})\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n\\n            #if !SNIPPET\\n            // Cleanup\\n            await agentsClient.DeleteAgentVersionAsync(agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;, agentVersion: agentVersion.Version.ToString());\\n            await agentsClient.DeleteAgentAsync(agentName: \\u0026#34;myStructuredInputAgent\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endif\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe README snippet placeholder (added in Step 1) uses concepts\\n3. ✅ Examples — **needs new sub-section for Structured Inputs**\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nOnly the `Examples` section needs updating to include the Unable to get tools for Hosted Agents in Foundry\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.AI.Projects (1.2.0-beta.5), Azure.AI.Projects.OpenAI (1.0.0-beta.5)\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nI have a hosted agent in Foundry. That hosted agent has some tools. When I try to get the list of tools associated with that hosted agent, I am not getting any tools back.\\n\\nHowever, this works with a prompt agent. I created a prompt agent and added web search tool to it. When I list the tools associated with the prompt agent, I get the tools list with web search tool in it.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nFor both hosted and prompt agents in Foundry, I should be able to get the list of tools attached/associated with that agent. Currently it only works with prompt agents.\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nFor hosted agents, tools are not returned.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\n```\\nvar projectEndpoint = \\\"https://my-foundry.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/my-project\\\";\\nvar agentId = \\\"my-hosted-agent\\\"\\n\\nvar projectClient = new AIProjectClient(\\n\\t\\tendpoint: new Uri(projectEndpoint),\\n\\t\\ttokenProvider: new DefaultAzureCredential());\\nvar agentRecordResult = await projectClient.Agents.GetAgentAsync(agentId);\\nvar response = agentRecordResult.GetRawResponse().Content.ToString();\\n```\\n\\nFor hosted agents, the response looks like this:\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\\"object\\\": \\\"agent\\\",\\n  \\\"id\\\": \\\"\\\"my-hosted-agent\\\",\\n  \\\"name\\\": \\\"\\\"my-hosted-agent\\\",\\n  \\\"versions\\\": {\\n    \\\"latest\\\": {\\n      \\\"metadata\\\": {},\\n      \\\"object\\\": \\\"agent.version\\\",\\n      \\\"id\\\": \\\"\\\"my-hosted-agent:4\\\",\\n      \\\"name\\\": \\\"\\\"my-hosted-agent\\\",\\n      \\\"version\\\": \\\"4\\\",\\n      \\\"description\\\": \\\"My
